identity.short_name: Kummer carry valuation sets lift depth; Gaussian root with 2-adic sign; Hensel to cubes and CRT

problem_spec.summary:
The lift depth k is defined by a p-adic valuation of a central binomial coefficient and computed via Kummer carry counting.
One local square root of -1 is seeded by a half-factorial (Wilson), another is constructed from a sum-of-two-squares representation (Gaussian integers),
with a 2-adic congruence selecting a canonical sign.
Both roots are Hensel-lifted to prime cubes and glued via CRT; the final output is the residue modulo 1000.
givens: Let $p$ and $q$ be distinct odd primes with $p\equiv 1\pmod{4}$ and $q\equiv 1\pmod{4}$.
Require that the base-$p$ addition $m+m=q-1$ has exactly three carries (so $k=3$).
Define $u$ to be the unique element of $\{\pm u_0\}$ satisfying $u\equiv 1\pmod{8}$.
Define $B$ as the unique integer with $0\le B<p^k$ such that $B\equiv ((p-1)/2)!\pmod{p}$ and $B^2\equiv -1\pmod{p^k}$.
Define $C$ as the unique integer with $0\le C<q^3$ such that $C\equiv u\pmod{q}$ and $C^2\equiv -1\pmod{q^3}$.
Define $r$ as the unique integer with $0\le r<M$ such that $r\equiv B\pmod{p^k}$ and $r\equiv C\pmod{q^3}$.
ask: Determine the unique $N\in\{0,1,\dots,999\}$ such that $N\equiv r\pmod{1000}$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: provides the half-factorial seed modulo $p$ and the prime-power modulus $p^3$

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: provides the odd prime whose cube modulus supports a Hensel lift of $x^2\equiv -1$

parameter(s).name: a
parameter(s).purpose: participates in the Gaussian-integer construction of a square root of $-1$ modulo $q$

parameter(s).name: b
parameter(s).purpose: participates in the Gaussian-integer construction of a square root of $-1$ modulo $q$

invariant(s).name: Kummer_carry_invariant_k_equals_3
invariant(s).statement: The number of base-$p$ carries in $m+m=q-1$ equals $k=v_p\binom{q-1}{m}$ and is fixed to be $3$.

invariant(s).name: half_Wilson_seed_root_mod_p
invariant(s).statement: For $p\equiv 1\pmod{4}$ prime, $((p-1)/2)!^2\equiv -1\pmod{p}$, giving a simple root seed for $x^2+1$.

invariant(s).name: Gaussian_root_from_sum_of_two_squares
invariant(s).statement: If $q=a^2+b^2$ then $u_0\equiv b\,a^{-1}\pmod{q}$ satisfies $u_0^2\equiv -1\pmod{q}$; the sign is fixed by a 2-adic selector.

invariant(s).name: two_adic_sign_selector_rigidity
invariant(s).statement: The auxiliary congruence $u\equiv 1\pmod{8}$ selects a unique sign among $\pm u_0\pmod{q}$.

invariant(s).name: hensel_unique_lifts_to_p_cubed_and_q_cubed
invariant(s).statement: For $f(x)=x^2+1$, each chosen seed is a simple root modulo the prime, hence lifts uniquely to $B\pmod{p^3}$ and $C\pmod{q^3}$.

invariant(s).name: bounded_quotient_controls_last_three_digits
invariant(s).statement: Writing $r=B+p^3 t$ with $0\le t<q^3$, the quotient $s$ in $p^3 t=(C-B)+q^3 s$ satisfies $0\le s<p^3$, enabling extraction of $t\bmod 8$ and hence $r\bmod 1000$.

invariant(s).name: CRT_gluing_rigidity
invariant(s).statement: Since $\gcd(p^3,q^3)=1$, CRT gives a unique $r\pmod{p^3 q^3}$; the bound $0\le r<M$ fixes the integer.

essential_assumption(s): The carry-pattern condition enforces $k=3$ and sets the lift depth to $p^3$.
The data $q=a^2+b^2$ with $\gcd(a,q)=1$ ensures a computable root of $-1$ modulo $q$.
The 2-adic congruence $u\equiv 1\pmod{8}$ fixes a canonical sign.
Simple-root conditions ensure uniqueness of Hensel lifts for $x^2+1$.
uniqueness_certificate(s):
Kummer's theorem forces $k$ from the base-$p$ carry pattern.
The half-factorial residue fixes a seed modulo $p$, and simple-root Hensel lifting makes $B\pmod{p^3}$ unique.
The Gaussian construction produces $\pm u_0\pmod{q}$, and the 2-adic selector fixes $u$; Hensel then makes $C\pmod{q^3}$ unique.
CRT yields a unique $r\pmod{p^3 q^3}$ and $0\le r<M$ fixes $r$; reducing modulo $1000$ fixes $N$ uniquely.

technique(s).name: Kummer_carry_valuation_for_binomial
technique(s).purpose: compute a p-adic valuation by counting carries, fixing the prime-power lift depth

technique(s).name: Legendre_digit_sum_bridge
technique(s).purpose: derive the digit-sum form used to interpret carries

technique(s).name: half_Wilson_square_root_mod_prime
technique(s).purpose: produce a canonical square root of $-1$ modulo $p$ using $((p-1)/2)!$

technique(s).name: Gaussian_root_constructor
technique(s).purpose: construct a square root of $-1$ modulo $q$ as $b\,a^{-1}$

technique(s).name: two_adic_sign_selector
technique(s).purpose: select a canonical root using an auxiliary congruence modulo $8$

technique(s).name: Hensel_lifting_setup
inputs: $f(x)=x^2+1$ and a simple root modulo a prime
technique(s).purpose: guarantee existence and uniqueness of lifts to prime cubes

technique(s).name: bounded_quotient_trick_for_mod_1000
inputs: the identity $p^3 t=(C-B)+q^3 s$ with bounds on $t$
technique(s).purpose: avoid full CRT arithmetic by forcing $0\le s<p^3$ and extracting $t\bmod 8$

technique(s).name: Chinese_remainder_reconstruction
technique(s).purpose: glue local prime-power solutions into a global residue class
outputs: unique $r$ and hence unique normalized output $N$

theorem(s).name: Legendre_formula_for_v_p_of_factorial
theorem(s).role: supports the digit-sum bridge for Kummer carry counting

theorem(s).name: Hensel_lemma_simple_root_lifting
theorem(s).role: provides unique lifting of a simple root to prime powers

theorem(s).name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
theorem(s).role: provides existence and uniqueness modulo coprime moduli

theorem(s).name: Gaussian_integer_norm_trick
theorem(s).role: yields a square root of $-1$ modulo $q$ from $q=a^2+b^2$

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Local-to-global reconstruction: build local roots (mod prime powers) and glue via CRT, with auxiliary congruences selecting canonical signs.

critical_trick(s): Use Kummer carry counting to force $k=3$ from a base-$5$ digit pattern.
Use $q=a^2+b^2$ plus a 2-adic condition to pin down a canonical root of $-1$ modulo $q$.
Use the bounded-quotient identity to extract $r\bmod 1000$ without computing the full CRT solution.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: exact integer arithmetic and binomial coefficients

task(s).name: correctness_verification
task(s).reason:
Check:
$k=v_5(\binom{148}{74})=3$,
$B=57$ satisfies $B\equiv2\pmod{5}$ and $B^2\equiv-1\pmod{125}$,
$u=105$ satisfies $u^2\equiv-1\pmod{149}$ and $u\equiv1\pmod{8}$,
$C^2\equiv-1\pmod{149^3}$ and $C\equiv u\pmod{149}$,
CRT gives $r\equiv57\pmod{125}$ and $r\equiv C\pmod{149^3}$,
and $N=r\bmod 1000$ equals $932$.
